subject: Ecology
title: Springtails and Mites
description: A quick, practical guide to Springtails and Mites.

hook.text: Springtails are tiny arthropods that launch themselves into the air to escape predators.

buildup.text: They graze on fungi, bacteria, and decomposing matter on the soil surface.

discovery.text: Soil mites are even more diverse—some are predators, some herbivores, some scavengers.

twist.text: A single square meter of forest floor can hold 100,000 mites from dozens of species.

climax.text: Together, springtails and mites fragment litter so bacteria and fungi can finish decomposition.

punchline.text: The soil's recycling crew is tiny but massive.

quiz.question: What ecological role do springtails play in soil?
options: They graze on fungi and fragment litter
